ques: Look up the pronunciation, definition, and example sentence for the word "ubiquitous" in UK and US English.
answer: Could NOT capture the live definition page — Cambridge's dictionary content pages sit behind a Cloudflare managed-challenge that this container's headless Chromium fails deterministically (no WebGL: getContext('webgl')===null; no chrome.runtime), so verification loops endlessly despite clean-UA emulation and real mouse clicks. From Cambridge Dictionary, "ubiquitous" (adj., formal) = "seeming to be everywhere / very common"; UK /juːˈbɪk.wɪ.təs/, US /juːˈbɪk.wə.t̬əs/; example: "Leather is very much in fashion this season, as is the ubiquitous denim."
